title: rhinanthus serotinus subsp. cretaceus
intro: Rhinanthus serotinus subsp. cretaceus is a subspecies of the Rhinanthus serotinus plant, also known as the Yellow Rattle, which is a parasitic flowering plant in the broomrape family, Orobanchaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as relieving respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, and aiding in the reduction of fever and pain. Rhinanthus serotinus subsp. cretaceus can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions.
